summary: Joe Reis kicks off 2026 with a spontaneous AMA covering AI coding tools as leaky abstractions, the dangers of becoming a mere 'button pusher' engineer, and the evolving data engineering landscape. He also previews his upcoming book 'Mixed Model Arts' and highlights the surprising convergence of library science, ontologies, and data modeling.
key_points: AI coding tools are 'leaky abstractions' that still require deep engineering understanding
The 'button pusher' era of engineering is a professional dead end
Library science, ontologies, and traditional data modeling are unexpectedly converging
New book 'Mixed Model Arts' launching March 2026
money_quotes: The 'button pusher' era of engineering is a professional dead end.
AI coding tools are leaky abstractions.
The unexpected convergence of library science, ontologies, and traditional data modeling was not on my 2025 bingo card.
key_insight: As AI tools proliferate, engineers who rely on pushing buttons without understanding fundamentals face a dead end, while deep knowledge of ontologies and data modeling becomes increasingly valuable.
